taxonomy: Malveopsis spicata is classified within the family Malvaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the mallow family. intro: Malveopsis spicata, also known as Spiked Mallow, is a perennial herb belonging to the Malvaceae family, characterized by its showy, pink or white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments such as digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
